steps.title: Identify the opposite outcomes
steps.content: The first sentence says single-ballot majoritarian systems tend to produce two-party systems. The next sentence says proportional-representation systems tend to support many parties, so the transition should mark an opposite relationship.

steps.title: Reject sequence and restatement
steps.content: Choice A suggests one event happens afterward, but the systems are being compared. Choice C would introduce an example of the first sentence, not an opposite system. Choice D would restate the same idea, which the second sentence does not do. The answer is B.
